description: Field-level and occupation-level signals about computing/quant career paths. Coverage is occupation- or field-level, never individual prediction.

items.title: Quant roles screen for demonstrated, legible skill signals
items.content: Quantitative trading and research roles tend to recruit on legible, demonstrated signals: competitive programming, probability/statistics fluency, measurable project outcomes, and structured interview performance. The pipeline rewards depth in a narrow stack early. This is an occupation-level pattern, not a guarantee for any individual.
items.citation: Industry hiring patterns; BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook (occupation-level framing)

items.title: Early internships function as the dominant on-ramp
items.content: In computing and quant fields, sophomore/junior internships are the primary conversion path to full-time offers. Timing matters: recruiting cycles for many quant and big-tech roles open well before the role start date. Missing a cycle can shift an on-ramp by roughly a year. Field-level pattern.

items.title: Narrow early specialization compounds but can reduce optionality
items.content: Deep early specialization (e.g., low-latency systems, statistical modeling) compounds fast within a track and raises legibility to specialized employers. The tradeoff is reduced lateral optionality: skills tuned to one niche transfer less cleanly to adjacent paths. This is a structural tradeoff, not a value judgment.
items.citation: Career-capital framing (So Good They Can't Ignore You; framework-level)

items.title: Credentials and portfolios trade off by sub-field
items.content: Some computing sub-fields weight legible credentials (degrees, named internships) more heavily; others weight demonstrable artifacts (shipped projects, open-source, competition results). Knowing which currency a target track uses changes where effort should go. Occupation-level.

items.title: High-intensity tracks carry a measurable emotional/time load
items.content: Recruiting-heavy and high-performance computing tracks concentrate stress into intense cycles (interview prep, on-call, deadlines). The load is real and front-loaded; sustainability depends on personal constraints the system cannot observe. Field-level pattern.
